title: GATA2 Variant Explorer
text:
Use the search box (top right) to search across all columns.
Use the column filters (under each header) to filter specific fields.
Helpful search terms to try:

Variant type: missense, nonsense, frameshift, splice_site, inframe_indel, inframe_ins, inframe_del
Protein region: N_tail, N_finger, spacer, C_finger, C_tail
Origin: germline, somatic, mixed
Database IDs: ClinVar, All_of_Us, gnomAD, COSMIC
HGVS: c., p., or a position like 345 or 354

Tip: You can combine terms in the global search (e.g., C_finger missense).

title: About GATA2
text:
GATA2 is an essential transcription factor that promotes hematopoietic stem and progenitor cell (HSPC) generation and function.

Figure 1: A. (Adapted from Bresnick and Johnson. Blood Adv. 2019.) The murine -77 and +9.5 transcriptional enhancers reside 77 and 9.5 kb upstream and downstream, respectively, of the Gata2 transcriptional start site. These enhancers are critical for mouse embryogenesis and hematopoiesis. The +9.5 enhancer confers hematopoietic stem cell (HSC) emergence at E10.5 in the AGM, and the -77 enhancer confers HSPC expansion and myelo-erythropoiesis at E14.5 in the fetal liver. B. GATA2 expression confers hematopoiesis. HSPC expansion impacted by GATA2 are highlighted in yellow.

title: About GATA2
section: GATA2 wild type vs 9 amino acid insertion variant
text:
GATA2 wild type vs 9 amino acid insertion variant
The GATA2 9–amino acid insertion variant formed the foundation of my doctoral work.

Figure 2: AlphaFold rendering comparing GATA2 wild-type and 9aa insertion variant.
This patient-derived in-frame insertion (p.A345delinsALLVAALLAA), located in the inter-zinc finger spacer region (Cavalcante de Andrade Silva et al. Leukemia. 2021), revealed how disruption of a transcription factor can drive genome-wide regulatory changes rather than isolated gene effects (Jung et al. J Clin Invest. 2023). Studying this variant led to the discovery that GATA2 decommissions inflammatory enhancers, linking regulatory dysfunction to immune dysregulation and blood cancer predisposition (Jung et al. Cell Rep. 2025).

title: Home
text: This site provides an educational and research-support resource for curated GATA2 variants, integrating evidence from public databases and the primary literature.

title: Home
text:
Mabel McCurdy (Minji Jung)


Hello! I am a Ph.D.-trained scientist with a background in functional genomics, transcriptional regulation, and variant interpretation, with a research focus on GATA2-associated disease mechanisms.
This project grew out of my doctoral work investigating how pathogenic GATA2 variants alter genome-wide regulatory programs, including enhancer dysregulation and inflammatory signaling. I created this resource to consolidate clinical and functional evidence on GATA2 variants and to further develop my skills in computational curation and clinical genomics–adjacent workflows.
I am actively pursuing industry roles in variant assessment and clinical genomics, where accurate interpretation and clear communication of genetic evidence directly impact patient care.

title: Home
text:
Disclaimer
This resource is for educational and research purposes only.
It is not intended for clinical diagnosis, treatment decisions, or patient management. Variant interpretations may evolve as new evidence becomes available. Because source materials occasionally contain inconsistencies or typographical errors, some entries may be incomplete or subject to revision. (Last updated Dec 2025.) —
